pi_items.source: setup_task.sh generates this exact path using ffmpeg to enforce 30s duration
verifier.py explicitly checks if "ambient" in rname or "loop" in rname
setup_task.sh ffmpeg uses -ar 44100 and verifier.py sets SAMPLE_RATE = 44100
verifier.py uses passed = score >= 70.0 and checks for back_half_swapped and front_half_swapped

privileged_info_summary: The task requires importing a 30-second audio file (/home/ga/Audio/import_me.wav) which is dynamically generated from real public domain recordings downloaded from the Internet Archive (such as Beethoven or LibriVox narrations). The agent must perform a split-and-swap to create a loop, specifically splitting the audio at 15.0 seconds (661500 samples at a 44100Hz sample rate) and overlapping the front half at 14.0 seconds (617400 samples). The verifier parses the Ardour session XML to confirm region offsets and positions fall within a 0.5-second tolerance (22050 samples), the track is renamed with keywords ambient or loop, and UI interaction is validated, demanding a minimum score of 70.
